title: Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: A classic Argentinian dish of savory beef empanadas, flaky crust filled with tender beef and spices.
instructions: Roll out the pastry dough to about 1/4-inch thickness.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the minced garlic, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Let the beef mixture cool slightly before spooning it onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the beef mixture and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
